name: loading
title: Loading
description: A page- or section-level spinner indicating that work is in progress. Use when content is fetching, a long-running action is pending, or a region of the UI must be visually blocked while waiting.

meta.docs:
# Loading

A page- or section-level spinner indicating that work is in progress. Use when content is fetching, a long-running action is pending, or a region of the UI must be visually blocked while waiting.

## When to use

- **Section loading** — content area fetching data. Use `<Spinner className="size-12" />` centered with `flex items-center justify-center`.
- **Full-page or section-blocking** — overlay the spinner: `<div className="absolute inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center bg-background/80 backdrop-blur-sm">` containing the Spinner.
- **Inline next to text** — drop `<Spinner />` (default `size-4`) next to a label like "Loading…".
- **Recolour to match context** — `<Spinner className="text-primary">` inherits any palette token via `currentColor`.
- **Custom screen-reader label** — pass `aria-label="Loading data"` to override the default `aria-label="Loading"`.

## When not to use

- **Inside a button or form field while pending** — use `InlineLoading` (small spinner with status text and state machine).
- **Skeleton placeholders for content shape** — use `Skeleton`, `SkeletonText`, or `SkeletonIcon`.
- **Indeterminate progress with a known step count** — use a `Progress` bar.

## Composition

- The wrapper re-exports the canonical `Spinner` from `spinner/`. Sizing, centering, and overlay are app-level composition — there is no `withOverlay` or `small` prop.
- For full-page blocking with scroll-lock, prefer wrapping the Spinner in a `<Dialog>` instead of building your own overlay.
- The default size is `size-4` (16px). Match Carbon's large 88px look with `size-16` or larger.
meta.migrationGuide:
# Loading — Carbon → shadcn migration guide ## Where it sits among the loading family Carbon ships two loading primitives: - **`InlineLoading`** — small spinner with status text + state machine (`active`/`finished`/`error`). Used next to buttons and form fields. (Covered separately.) - **`Loading`** — the bigger, page/section-level spinner. Optional dark overlay. **This guide.** shadcn ships **one** spinner primitive (`Spinner`) and you compose sizing, overlay, and layout yourself. Same canonical source as `inline-loading`. ## Mapping | Concept | Carbon `Loading` | shadcn | |---|---|---| | Default | 88×88 spinner positioned absolute-center | `` (or whatever size) | | Small | `small` prop (16×16) | `` (default `size-4` = 16px) | | Overlay | `withOverlay` (full-screen dark backdrop) | compose absolute positioned `
` + center the Spinner | | Active vs. inactive | `active` prop (defaults true) | conditional render: `{isLoading && }` | | `description` (a11y label) | prop | `aria-label` on the parent or `` next to the spinner; default `aria-label="Loading"` already on `` | ## Carbon features missing in shadcn - **`withOverlay`** — Carbon ships a dark full-screen overlay primitive. shadcn requires manual composition: ```tsx
``` Wrap in `fixed inset-0` for full-page; `absolute inset-0` inside a positioned ancestor for section-level. - **`active={false}` to render an "off" state** — Carbon keeps the spinner mounted and stops the animation. shadcn: just don't render the Spinner when not loading. - **`small` size variant** — Carbon's binary toggle. shadcn: just `` (16px) vs. `` (48px) etc. - **`description`** (a11y) — Carbon's SR-only label. shadcn's `` already has `aria-label="Loading"`; override via `aria-label` prop or wrap with custom ``. - **Carbon's specific spinner animation** — Carbon uses a dual-arc CSS animation matching IBM's design language. shadcn uses Lucide's `Loader2Icon` with `animate-spin` (single full rotation). Visually different. - **Built-in centering inside a positioned container** — Carbon's spinner is `position: absolute` and self-centers. shadcn: you center it (typically with `flex items-center justify-center` on the parent). - **Skeleton variant (``)** — Carbon ships skeleton states. shadcn has only the generic `skeleton` primitive. ## shadcn features missing in Carbon - **`` works inline anywhere** — drop it into buttons, badges, tiles. Carbon's `Loading` is full/block-level by design. - **Tailwind size composability** — ``, `size-4`, `size-6`, `size-8`, `size-12`, `size-16`. Continuous sizing instead of two fixed sizes. - **Recolour via `currentColor`** — `` recolours to whichever palette token. Carbon's spinner is fixed (typically blue). ## Behavioural differences - **Default size** — Carbon: 88px (large) or 16px (small). shadcn: 16px (`size-4`); explicitly resize for full-page use. - **Animation rhythm** — Carbon: ~750ms full rotation, custom dual-arc. shadcn: ~1000ms full rotation, single full circle. - **Colour** — Carbon: blue interactive token. shadcn: inherits `currentColor`. - **Z-index of overlay** — Carbon's overlay: high (above page content). shadcn: you set `z-50` (or whatever) explicitly. - **Scroll-lock when overlay is shown** — Carbon: doesn't lock body scroll. shadcn: doesn't lock body scroll either. If you need it (full-page blocking), add `useEffect(() => { document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'; return () => { document.body.style.overflow = '' } }, [])` to your overlay component or use a `` instead. ## Migration checklist 1. Decide whether you need the overlay or not. 2. Without overlay (most common — e.g., "loading content in a section"): - Replace `` with `` (or a size that matches Carbon's 88px → use `size-16` or larger). - Wrap in `
` for centering. 3. With overlay (full-page or section-blocking): ```tsx
``` Use `fixed` instead of `absolute` if you want page-wide; ensure the parent is `relative` for `absolute` to work. 4. For `small`: drop the prop, use `` at default size (16px). 5. For `active={false}`: stop rendering the Spinner — `{isLoading && }`. 6. For `description="Loading data"`: pass `aria-label="Loading data"` to ``. 7. For full-page blocking with scroll-lock: consider switching to a shadcn `` with a Spinner inside instead — that's the right primitive for "block the page until done". 8. For Carbon's specific dual-arc animation: not preserved. If brand-required, write your own SVG spinner; otherwise accept the Lucide `Loader2Icon` look.
